methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Last Energy, founded by Bret Kugelmass, is a Series C nuclear energy company that has raised $100M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy: three public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and anchors the analysis. Using the $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C progression to the next financing stage yields $170M. The Peer-Median approach is not reliable here: while eight same-sector comps exist, insufficient overlap between same-stage peers, adjacent-stage peers, and similar valuation tiers prevents meaningful triangulation—fewer than three datapoints pass the narrow filter—making sector-wide median comparison misleading.

The triangulated valuation range is $144M to $195M, with a median of $170M, derived from a single applicable method. This narrow methodology base and limited peer comparability represent material uncertainty. The valuation is particularly sensitive to the stage-progression multiplier assumption and to whether public nuclear names trade at relevant multiples to private frontier-stage peers. Further validation would benefit from direct disclosure of Last Energy's current revenue, burn rate, and technical milestones relative to named comparables.
